bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting my maximum-utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer is all valueC, which is the lowest utility for me on every issue, so I should anchor with my preferred values across the domain.

I am targeting a very high utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.02, and the opponent has not yet shown any concession beyond repeating an all-valueC offer. To avoid simply repeating my prior all-valueA bid while making only a small concession, I am yielding on a low-weight issue and holding firm on the most important issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while making only small concessions. The opponent has improved their offer slightly on issueA and issueB but still gives low value on most issues, so I will hold firm on the most important issues and concede only low-weight issues to avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is no need for large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved for me from all valueC to a bid worth about 0.52, suggesting some willingness to concede, but they are still far from my preferred outcomes. I am varying my prior offer by conceding on lower-weight issues E and F while holding firm on the more important issues A, B, and C.

The opponent’s offers are steadily improving for me, from very low utility to a latest offer around 0.64, so they appear to be conceding. Since it is still early at t=0.08, I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.88, holding firm on the most important issues A/B/C while offering moderate concessions on lower-weight issues D/E/F.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.84 because it is still very early at t=0.10, so only modest concessions are warranted. The opponent has improved from all valueC to some valueA/valueB offers, especially on issueB and issueC, but their latest offer is not clearly better than their prior one, so I will hold firm on my highest-weight issues and concede mainly on low-weight issueF.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.80 while making a meaningful early concession on low-weight issueD rather than weakening the high-weight issues A, B, and C. The opponent’s offers have steadily improved for me, especially their latest bid, so at t=0.12 I can hold firm on my most important issues while moving closer on lower-priority ones.
